danger_signals.visual_form: Castle architecture and soldiers bearing castle insignia
danger_signals.meaning: Military threat; hostile authority controlling the province

danger_signals.visual_form: Dragon enemy in fire-themed arena within quarry/ruins
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard and guardian obstacle; supernatural danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Darkened fortress aesthetic, demonic visual language
danger_signals.meaning: Sealed evil breaking containment; existential threat to region

danger_signals.visual_form: Empty Redmont; abandoned buildings; absence of NPCs
danger_signals.meaning: Hostage crisis; breakdown of civic safety

danger_signals.visual_form: Character positioning as antagonist; physical attacks
danger_signals.meaning: Betrayal and internal threat; corruption within trusted circles

safety_signals.visual_form: Village with NPCs, shops, accessible buildings
safety_signals.meaning: Safe hub; friendly civilization; quest origin point

safety_signals.visual_form: Mayor character in central locations; dialogue-based guidance
safety_signals.meaning: Legitimate local leadership; trustworthy information source

safety_signals.visual_form: Allied character physically present in key moments
safety_signals.meaning: Trusted ally; protective presence; rescue capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Isolated wise NPC offering direction and training knowledge
safety_signals.meaning: Hidden sanctuary; wisdom resource; preparation point
Quest progress; restoration of protective seal; player agency in solution

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Armored soldiers, castle knights, armed forces bearing castle colors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle architecture; fortified positions; soldier patrols in conquered areas

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Civilian clothing; mayor's regalia on Edgar
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Not enemies; vulnerable civilians shown as hostages
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Village buildings; shops; town square gathering places

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Religious vestments on Bishop Garland; Father Pierre in clerical garb
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Bishop Garland as combat antagonist; clergy complicit in seal-weakening plot
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Religious architecture; sacred sites tied to statue locations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Galbalan boss entity; dark creatures in fortress realm
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortress island; sealed cave systems; corrupted landscape zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Village appears populated; shops operational; NPCs conduct daily business
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Townspeople move freely; Edgar provides governance; social structures intact

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased soldier presence in towns; fortification signals; Edgar appears stressed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs express concern; militia movements visible; authority figures show tension

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental corruption; fortress grows darker/more prominent; statue importance emphasized
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Urgency in NPC dialogue; Chester becomes hostile; Chester's sacrifice need emerges

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Town appears abandoned; buildings empty; visual absence replaces population
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Remaining citizens hide; no commerce; castle soldiers occupy key locations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fortress fully manifest; demonic realm visible; Elena endangered; stakes maximized visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All factions converge; sacrifice becomes necessary; Elena's vulnerability shown directly

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fortress sinks into sea; world returns to normal lighting; Redmont restored
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Townspeople return; Elena's emotional state shown (tears at farewell); Adol departs as hero

environmental_storytelling.example: Quarry where Mayor Edgar is imprisoned shows signs of forced labor; suggests prolonged hostage situation without explicit exposition
Statues distributed across diverse locations (quarry, ruins, mountain, hidden fortress) visually communicates distributed protection mechanism; player retrieval mirrors original hero's actions
Empty Redmont contrasts sharply with earlier populated village; visual emptiness communicates crisis without dialogue requirement
Chester appears both as ally (early meetings) and antagonist (statue confrontation), with physical positioning showing his internal conflict and eventual heroic redemption through sacrifice
Galbalan's fortress uses demonic visual language distinct from castle stone, church architecture, and village timber; visual language alone communicates supernatural threat category
Fire dragon guarding statue in quarry; encounter difficulty and environment type visually reinforce 'seal protection' concept without explicit explanation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stands in town center; provides quests; offers guidance; appears in formal positions
Found imprisoned in quarry; requires rescue; unable to provide leadership
Encounters player in ruins; appears to be gathering statues for protection
Physically attacks Adol; attempts to seize statues; shows confusion about true loyalties
Sacrifices self to sink island; completes sealing ritual; shown at end in conversation before sacrifice
Confronted as boss enemy; actively weakening seal through conspiracy
Seeks statues for power; threatens town; redeems himself after defeat, showing capacity for repentance
Presence implied through weakening manifestations; communicates telepathically; appears weakened by seal
Directly abducts Elena; makes demands; becomes final boss; demonstrates overwhelming power
Provides exposition about statue purpose; offers guidance
Abducted by Galbalan; shown as hostage; emotional vulnerability emphasized
Admits love to Dogi at night; cries at farewell; shown in close emotional proximity to Adol
Appears at critical rescue moments; provides physical assistance; offers emotional support
Appears isolated on mountain; offers direction; provides training knowledge; validates player progression

notes: Ys III uses side-scrolling platformer presentation to manifest world-state changes through environmental design and NPC positioning rather than top-down overview. Danger signals emphasize military threat (Valestein), supernatural threat (Galbalan), and internal threat (Chester's corruption). Safety signals cluster around Redmont's civic structures and trusted allies. Faction identity relies more on NPC clustering and architectural style than explicit color-coding, reflecting 1991 SNES technical constraints. The narrative structure (three-act: threat detection, hostage crisis, final confrontation) maps directly to visual environmental transformation: populated village → abandoned village → demonic fortress. World-state visibility depends heavily on NPC presence/absence and building accessibility rather than dramatic weather or lighting changes.
sources_used: wikipedia - Ys III: Wanderers from Ys (plot, gameplay, and character information)
